Interactive/browser login shipped: engine DeviceState::NeedsLogin(url) → State::NeedsLogin + auth_url in StatusReport; tnet up (no authkey) polls + prints the login URL; tnet status shows it. Live-verified: URL surfaced, status instant mid-registration. Also fixed a latent hang: status() now reads cheap device_state() and only does the blocking netmap query when Running (was hanging behind the busy control actor during NeedsLogin).

status --watch shipped: Request::Watch streams StatusReport over the LocalAPI; CLI 'tnet status --watch'. Backend lifecycle watch<generation> channel + per-device DeviceState receiver in a two-level epoch loop (architect Option 2) so a watcher survives down+up and a watch started before the first up follows the device. Snapshot-first rule (changed()=wake-only) per verified tokio watch semantics; lock never held across changed(). Live-verified: watch-before-up streamed NoState→NeedsLogin.
Superseded: bumped daemon straight to engine v0.6.9 (991c8c95) which includes the v0.6.8 IPv4-only netmap fix plus the v0.6.9 systematic null-tolerance. No separate v0.6.8 bump needed.
probe, not a real issue

RESOLVED as verify+guard+docs. Engine does leak-safe DNS takeover in TUN mode (only OS-wide mode): OS resolver->in-datapath MagicDNS->exit peerAPI DoH over overlay (fresh socket, v4-only). Netstack mode = no OS resolver touched = no leak surface. Dangerous 'OS-wide exit DNS-leaking' config is UNREACHABLE by construction. Shipped: build_config warn! when exit_node set w/o TUN + documented invariant (ipn.rs module doc) + prefs.rs DNS-fallback/advertise notes. Stale dep edges to tsd-lfz/m8s removed (engine provides per-OS DNS, not the daemon).
Engine-provided: ts_host_net macOS scutil DNS programming works in TUN mode (validated live, ENGINE_ASKS #6 region). Daemon does not re-implement per-OS DNS. Closing as satisfied by engine.
Shipped v0.6.0: tnet file cp/list/get over Taildrop (Request::FileCp/FileList/FileGet, diag.rs), path/dest hardened in v0.7.x. 86 lib tests.
Shipped v0.5.0: Tailscale SSH server (ssh cargo feature, spawn-on-install in finish_up, fail-closed listen_ssh, root privilege-drop). --ssh/--no-ssh on up+set.

Engine shipped ZeroizeOnDrop + no-Copy private keys (geiserx_tailscale v0.11.0). Daemon impact NONE — never holds raw private key (SecretString + new_with_secret + by-ref PersistState read). Pure pin bump pending.

MOOT as conceived. Interactive 'tnet up' (no authkey → surface login URL + wait for Running) is ALREADY fully implemented via status-polling: tnet.rs interactive_up gate + poll_for_auth_url + wait_for_running + the 'To authenticate this node, visit:' print, and status()/whoami already surface auth_url (extracted from the engine DeviceState NeedsLogin). watch_ipn_bus/browse_to_url would only RE-implement this working capability with a different engine primitive — no user-visible gain (Go's WatchIPNBus is an internal optimization; the CLI poll is functionally equivalent). Closing per the no-marginal-batches discipline; reopen only if a concrete polling inadequacy surfaces.

Already shipped (verified): wait_decision returns WaitStep::Failed(reason) on s.error.is_some() (tnet.rs:2779-2780), so wait_for_running bails fast on a terminal error mid-wait with the specific reason. StatusReport.error exists + has failed-case tests. Closed in the batch that added the wait_decision classifier; bead was stale.

Already shipped (verified): --accept-risk=lose-ssh is fully implemented for both up (tnet.rs:159-165) and set (244-249) — is_risk_accepted + the ssh-toggle/force-reauth-over-Tailscale-SSH refusal (batches 27-28, beads tsd-eqx et al). The bead's premise ('this fork has NO --accept-risk machinery') is outdated. Daemon-CLI refuses non-interactively rather than Go's y/N TTY prompt — a faithful non-interactive equivalent. Stale; closing.
Shipped in PR #112 (abfbab9): file_cp source open + file_get dest create now use O_NOFOLLOW, closing the stat->open TOCTOU symlink-swap window, matching debug_capture. ELOOP regression test added.

PROVEN LIVE on the Mac Mini (real mango-alpha.ts.net tailnet, 2026-06-12). tnet status shows relay='mad' (Madrid DERP region) for relayed peers + direct 192.168.10.11:41641 for the direct peer — the StatusNode.relay field (engine v0.28.2 #89) populated, mutually exclusive with cur_addr. Both human + --json views. Userspace mode, NEV undisturbed.

ABSORBED BY ENGINE — not daemon-lane work. The engine's ts_host_net/macos.rs (13.5KB, mirrors Go router_userspace_bsd.go: /sbin/route + scutil, split-default for exit nodes, rollback-on-partial, RAII teardown) programs the macOS routing table + resolver, wired AUTOMATICALLY into the TUN datapath (ts_runtime/tun_actor.rs host_net()+apply_routes/apply_dns), crash-safe via impl Drop for HostGuard. The engine facade exposes NO host_net seam (0 hits) — the daemon CANNOT and must not program routes (doing so = double-FIB-programming + conflicting teardown = harmful). Already live-validated on the Mac Mini (ENGINE_ASKS.md: filed the /usr/sbin/route->/sbin/route bug, engine fixed it v0.6.10). The daemon's only TUN-platform role (shipped): select TransportMode::Tun + the lowest_free_utun name default + the root preflight (config.rs already documents 'Nothing for the daemon to add'). Bead was filed under the classic Go layering (daemon owns wgengine/router); this fork put the router INSIDE the engine.

ABSORBED BY ENGINE — same as tsd-jys. The engine's ts_host_net/linux.rs programs the Linux host routing/netfilter/DNS in TUN mode, wired into ts_runtime/tun_actor.rs, gated on TransportMode::Tun, RAII teardown. No daemon-lane router seam on the facade. The daemon selects TUN mode + does the root preflight; the engine does netlink/nftables/policy-routing. Not daemon work.

DONE (stale bead — implemented in a later session, never closed). The engine shipped Device::rebind() in v0.12.0 (ask #4); the daemon consumes it: src/ipn/linkmon.rs (the LinkSnapshot poll-based OS link-change monitor — every POLL_INTERVAL, diffs host non-loopback/non-v6-link-local addrs; on change calls Device::rebind()) + the monitor loop in src/ipn/mod.rs:99. The 2026-06-08 NOTES ('engine exposes no rebind, defer') predate the v0.12.0 rebind shipment + my current pin (v0.28.2 has it). Gated-e2e covered. tsd-0u9 (launchd, which this blocked) is unblocked.
